mechanism_narrative: Declines in the federal funds rate reduce corporate borrowing costs and lower equity discount rates, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.02 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns show no statistical correlation with FEDFUNDS changes.

mechanism_narrative: A monthly increase in the unemployment rate exceeding 0.3 percentage points signals deteriorating labor market conditions that trigger immediate risk-off positioning, producing negative S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 5-day returns in > 55% of instances where UNRATE rises > 0.3pp.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous expansion in real personal consumption expenditures and housing starts indicates synchronized consumer and capital demand growth, driving positive S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Joint positive growth in RSAFS and HOUST fails to predict positive 60-day SPX returns with direction accuracy below 50%.

mechanism_narrative: Accelerating industrial production growth during periods of low inflation compresses input cost pressures and boosts corporate margins, yielding positive S&P 500 forward returns over 20 trading days.
failure_condition: INDPRO acceleration coincides with negative 20-day SPX returns when CPIAUCSL growth exceeds 2.5% annually.

mechanism_narrative: A quarterly contraction in real GDP growth below 1.0% annualized triggers recession fears that immediately depress equity valuations, causing negative S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX forward 5-day returns remain positive in > 60% of quarters where GDPC1 growth falls below 1.0%.

mechanism_narrative: Rising nonfarm payrolls combined with a stable or declining federal funds rate indicates accommodative policy supporting labor demand, generating positive S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: The joint signal of rising PAYEMS and falling FEDFUNDS yields negative 60-day SPX returns in > 55% of observations.

mechanism_narrative: High baseline unemployment above 5.0% creates a buffer against inflationary wage pressures, allowing sustained equity market rallies that produce positive S&P 500 returns over 20 trading days.
failure_condition: UNRATE above 5.0% coincides with negative 20-day SPX returns in > 60% of monthly observations.

mechanism_narrative: Month-over-month declines in the index of industrial production reflect weakening manufacturing activity that immediately reduces near-term earnings expectations, driving negative S&P 500 returns over 5 trading days.
failure_condition: INDPRO declines fail to predict negative 5-day SPX returns with direction accuracy below 45% across rolling 1-year windows.
